title: More Nonsense Limerick 36
content:
There was an old person of Woking,
    Whose mind was perverse and provoking;
    He sate on a rail, 
    With his head in a pail,
    That illusive old person of Woking.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "More Nonsense Limerick 36", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
